stem: A pupil types `www.bbc.co.uk` into a web browser and the BBC home page appears.
model_answer: The Domain Name System (DNS) translates the human-readable domain name (www.bbc.co.uk) into the IP address of the server that hosts the page. The browser asks a DNS server for the address, the DNS server replies, and the browser then sends an HTTP/HTTPS request to that IP address.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award one mark for the translation idea (domain → IP), one for the request/lookup interaction. Mentioning HTTP is a bonus, not required.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe the role of the DNS in fetching the page.

mark_points.text: DNS translates a domain name into an IP address
the browser queries a DNS server before sending its HTTP request
